animal-tree-1.png.description: A decision tree representing a search for a species of animal.
animal-tree-1.png.source: Created by the Bootstrap team in Draw.io

dot-plot-pounds.png.description: A dot plot showing the distribution of pounds across a small dataset of animals. Each dot is an emoij of the species for each animal

dot-plot-tail.png.description: A dot plot showing the distribution of tail-values (true and false) across a small dataset of animals. Each dot is an emoij of the species for each animal

empty-tree.png.description: An empty decision tree representing a search for a species of animal.
empty-tree.png.source: Created by the Bootstrap team in Draw.io

final-tree.png.description: A decision tree that predicts if individuals will buy a video game

optimal-animals-tree.png.description: A decision tree with two levels, which tries to classify an animal based on the value of three boolean columns (swims, tail, and mammal)
optimal-animals-tree.png.source: Created by the Bootstrap team in Draw.io

tail-classifier-code.png.description: the code for a Pyret function that checks if an animal's row has a tail, producing 'cat' if it does and 'snail' if it doesn't

tail-classifier-tree.png.description: An decision tree representing a search for a species of animal. The root node splits based on who has a tail. The 'no' branch has 2 tarantulas and 2 snails, while the 'yes' branch has 2 lizards, 4 dogs, and 5 cats
tail-classifier-tree.png.source: Created by the Bootstrap team in Draw.io

tree-1.png.description: A portion of a decision tree, showing the number of students who do and don't have glasses
tree-1.png.source: Created by the Bootstrap team in Draw.io

tree-2.png.description: A portion of a decision tree representing a search for a student in class, showing the number of students who do and don't have glasses, do and don't have red hair, and are and aren't girls. Ultimately this results in identifying the student 'Jimmy'
tree-2.png.source: Created by the Bootstrap team in Draw.io

tree-3.png.description: A portion of a decision tree representing a search for a student in class. This one has multiple branches and levels, and leaf nodes for 'Jimmy' and 'Susan'
tree-3.png.source: Created by the Bootstrap team in Draw.io

terminology-tree.png.description: A decision tree used to introduce vocabulary (node, root node, leaf)
